bullets.text: The US has announced it will cease funding HIV programmes in South Africa, citing concerns over the protection of the white-minority Afrikaner community.
A global framework for reparatory justice has been adopted at a conference in Ghana, aiming to ensure fair compensation for those affected by legacies of enslavement and to address debt burdens.

The CDC plans to allocate $107 million in emergency funding to support the Ebola response efforts in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda, as the number of infected individuals surpasses 1,000.
Gold Coast council is considering the return of ocean pools to its shoreline after strong community interest, decades after they were last present.
A Sydney startup has been linked to one of the world's largest AI pornography sites, with experts raising concerns about the presence of child abuse material.
Polling indicates that voters support the LNP's preparations for the 2032 Games, but there is opposition to the government's broken promise regarding a stadium in Victoria Park.

Oil prices have fallen after Israel and Hizbollah agreed to a ceasefire, raising hopes for a broader agreement between the US and Iran. However, central banks remain cautious about global inflation despite the drop in energy prices.
Cuba has passed urgent reforms to liberalise its economy under pressure from the US, marking potentially the biggest shake-up since the early years of the revolution in the 1960s.

Canada's financial regulator has lowered capital requirements for the country's largest banks for the first time in three years, aiming to boost lending and support a domestic push for economic growth.
